news.title: Madras HC issues notice to Tamil Nadu on PIL against layout approvals in hill areas
news.para: An activist has approached the Madras High Court challenging a Tamil Nadu government order. This order empowers urban planning authorities to approve housing layouts in hill areas. It bypasses the mandatory clearance from the Hill Area Conservation Authority (HACA). The petitioner argues this dilutes environmental safeguards and could cause ecological damage.

news.title: Under smart city mission, over 357 projects worth over ₹11,000 crore completed in Gujarat
news.para: With an extensive network of roads and railways, along with the development of international airports and ports, Gujarat today has a well-developed transport network that provides excellent connectivity to its citizens. To manage the increasing traffic in urban areas efficiently, overbridges and underbridges are being constructed across the state. In particular, in the four major cities, Ahmedabad, Rajkot, Vadodara, and Surat, a strong road network is being developed through such infrastructure, making transportation easier for citizens.

news.title: About 34% of 72 lakh Karnataka homes don't have tap water: CAG
news.para: Karnataka has fallen significantly short of its tap water connection targets under the Jal Jeevan Mission, with 24.5 lakh rural households still lacking access past the March 2024 deadline. A CAG report highlights major planning, execution, and financial management flaws, impacting water quality monitoring and project completion.

news.title: Lucknow development body to launch OTS scheme, set up desk to help defaulters
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority launches a one-time settlement scheme from April 18, offering a waiver of penal interest and a 2% rebate for full payment within 30 days. A dedicated help desk will assist applicants, with applications to be processed within three months. The scheme covers various property types, including residential, commercial, and institutional allotments.

news.title: Over 250 projects completed in Kanpur in past nine years: Minister
news.para: Kanpur has seen significant development with 252 projects completed since 2017, costing over Rs 1,500 crore. These include housing, sanitation, healthcare, education, and infrastructure like the Metro and Ring Road. The state has also witnessed a decline in crime and growth in investment and industrial development.

news.title: Chandigarh administration caught in a policy conundrum over land pooling versus outright acquisition
news.para: Chandigarh faces a land development dilemma, caught between central pressure for optimization and farmer demands. While land pooling faced technical hurdles, direct acquisition is now favored financially, promising significant government revenue despite farmer protests for a participatory model. This highlights a complex balancing act for the administration.

news.title: Ludhiana: Over 1,500 scramble for a spot in LIT's Atal Apartment Scheme
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust has received a strong response for the remaining 179 flats under the Atal Apartment Scheme, with a draw tentatively scheduled for April 14. Officials have uploaded the applicant list online, with some requiring pending documents.

news.title: Maharashtra decentralises land regularisation powers, collectors, commissioners to handle 90% of cases
news.para: Maharashtra's legislative assembly has unanimously passed a new bill. This law empowers district officials to resolve most land-use violations locally. This decentralization aims to speed up processes and reduce citizen burdens. Previously, many cases went to the state secretariat, causing delays. The amendment provides a clear legal framework for these regularizations.

news.title: One lakh houses will be handed over to beneficiaries by March 31: Telangana minister
news.para: Telangana is set to deliver over two lakh Indiramma houses by June. The government plans to sanction a second phase of housing in April. Funds will be allocated for incomplete houses from the previous regime. Sarpanchs will chair village housing committees, with Collectors reviewing proposals. The state aims to provide a house for every eligible poor person.

news.title: Apartments in Bengaluru caught dumping sewage into lake
news.para: Pollution board cracks down on apartments lacking sewage treatment plants as untreated waste continues entering Shikaripalya Lake, worsening environmental and public health concerns.

news.title: No clarity on property tax, Ghaziabad residents fear 12% penalty on dues from FY27
news.para: Consumers in Noida face uncertainty over property tax as the financial year ends, with conflicting statements from the mayor and GMC officials regarding a tax hike rollback. Residents fear a 12% penalty if they don't pay by March 31, while the government is still considering options after a court decision.

news.title: Telangana government plans reallocation as over 12,000 2BHK houses lie vacant
news.para: The Telangana government is addressing the issue of unoccupied 2BHK housing units, with 12,000 homes remaining vacant, primarily due to their distance from beneficiaries. Notices have been issued, and a deadline extension is in place, with reallocation planned for those who still cannot occupy their allotted houses. Future projects will prioritize proximity to former slum areas.

news.title: Godrej Industries arm buys office space worth ₹365 crore in Mumbai's Vikhroli
news.para: Godrej Seeds and Genetics Ltd has acquired three floors in Godrej One, Vikhroli, for Rs 366 crore. This intra-group consolidation of 70,361 sq ft of office space highlights a trend of corporates optimizing real estate for long-term efficiency. The deal underscores Vikhroli's growing prominence as a business hub.

news.title: UltraTech Cement settles JAL dispute, agrees to redeem ₹1,000-crore shares
news.para: UltraTech Cement has settled a ₹1,000 crore arbitration with Jaiprakash Associates (JAL) by agreeing to redeem preference shares tied to the Dalla Super cement asset. This resolution aids Adani Group's takeover of JAL, reducing their upfront cash outgo and directing proceeds to lenders.

news.title: Puravankara plans 30 project launches with ₹55,000 crore GDV over next two years
news.para: Puravankara is set to launch 30 residential projects in South India and Mumbai within the next two years, representing a gross development value of over ₹55,000 crore. This expansion, covering 51.14 million sq ft, is driven by sustained demand in the residential market.

news.title: ArisInfra Solutions approves merger of subsidiary ArisUnitern RE Solutions
news.para: ArisInfra Solutions has approved the amalgamation of its subsidiary, ArisUnitern RE Solutions, with the parent company, effective April 1, 2026. This integration aims to enhance operational efficiency and unlock cross-selling opportunities. ArisInfra shareholders will receive shares in exchange for their holdings in ArisUnitern.

news.title: Women owners in Pune to get 50% property tax discount
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) has disappointed small property owners by not announcing a blanket tax waiver as promised. Instead, only women property owners will receive a 50% tax discount, a move civic activists have criticized as a betrayal of electoral promises. The civic body aims to generate revenue through an amnesty scheme for unauthorized constructions and by recovering outstanding dues.

news.title: Visakhapatnam civic body's property tax collections cross ₹510 crore
news.para: Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation has achieved a record ₹510 crore in property tax collections for the 2025-26 fiscal year, surpassing last year's ₹500 crore. Officials anticipate reaching ₹600 crore by March 31, boosted by a 50% state government interest waiver on pending dues and focused efforts on vacant land tax and arrears.

news.title: Illegal sand mining flourishes along Saberi river bed in Koraput
news.para: Large-scale illegal sand mining is rampant along the Saberi riverbed in Koraput district, with tipper trucks openly transporting sand in violation of norms. Activists allege a nexus between sand mafias and officials, with seized sand being regularized through spot auctions, bypassing regulations and causing ecological damage.

news.title: Sand shortage persists as mining auctions delayed in Rajasthan
news.para: Sand remains scarce and unaffordable in Rajasthan despite fresh auction steps, with procedural hurdles and environmental clearances significantly slowing the process.  The Rajasthan High Court's intervention, questioning the state's mining practices and the absence of scientific assessment for natural replenishment, further complicates operationalization of legal mining sites, leading to continued illegal transportation and high prices.
